text.title: reasoning-first-architectures Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for reasoning-first-architectures as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using reasoning models for high-throughput classification (thousands of calls per minute).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reasoning models have higher latency (5-30s per call) and cost (10-50x). At high throughput, reasoning cost becomes prohibitive and latency makes the system unusable. Use standard models with classification prompts; escalate to reasoning models only for edge cases flagged by the classifier.

failure-mode.description: Streaming DeepSeek R1 think tags directly to users.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DeepSeek R1 think tags contain the model's internal monologue including potentially confidential input echoing and intermediate reasoning that should not be visible to end users. Always strip <think> blocks before returning output.
